title: Manage retained history and recovery
preconditions: The requested operation uses an owner-only approved target, compatible version, actor attribution, bounded input, and passes path and permission validation.
postconditions: Successful maintenance has one attributable complete outcome; failure leaves the previously authoritative store and surviving report history unchanged.
main_flow: 1. INSIGHTS previews the affected retained identities and dependency consequences for destructive history operations. 2. An explicit confirmed deletion follows the documented dependency rule and records attributable outcome evidence without rewriting surviving reports. 3. Backup captures all durable INSIGHTS state with a manifest, fingerprint, version, permissions, and integrity evidence. 4. Verification independently checks completeness and integrity. 5. Restore builds and verifies a separate candidate, then atomically activates it only after all checks pass. 6. Status returns metadata-only version, integrity, retention counts, and recovery evidence.
alt_flows: Deletion without explicit confirmation, with unresolved dependent restatements, or beyond bounded scope is rejected.
Unsafe path, symlink traversal, wrong ownership or permissions, incompatible version, corruption, omission, insufficient space, or failed integrity check blocks backup or restore activation.
Cancellation or crash before activation leaves the prior store authoritative.
Status and failures never emit report contents, amounts, merchant text, or private paths.
metadata: {"depth_tier":3,"primary_actor":"P1","trigger":"The owner explicitly requests report deletion, backup, verification, restore, or storage-status evidence.","status":"draft"}
